tax: Placed at genus rank, Latticeling is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a hollow self-templating quartz shell. A new species belongs here only if it shows a hollow ringing quartz body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a clustered geode on crystal stilts. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a bell ring and scattered glints — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Latticeling occupies vug-lined crystal galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where silica seep crystallises a hollow shell and recur with the vug re-saturates, sustaining themselves on silica supersaturation.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: rings and grows blades to noise. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: hollow ringing shell and refraction. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: shard-growth and disorienting tone. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Latticeling matters because kills careless crystal crews. It lets survey reports sort hollow ringing forms from solid ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Latticeling is the genus-rank grouping of hollow resonant quartz-shell elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a hollow faceted shell with a ringing core and outward shard-growth, and divide below into species groups by crystal colour and core volume. Recognised by its bell-like ring when struck.
